behaviors.description: The wait time between retries doubles with each consecutive failure, up to a configured maximum.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior is pure backoff math/state progression and can fail inside one retry-policy function/object. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess is required to expose wrong doubling or cap logic. MOCK-HIDE: mocks do not hide this; deterministic inputs catch it. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: higher levels were considered due to broader API context, but behavior text is limited to timing progression ('doubles ... up to a configured maximum'), which disqualifies System/Agentic/Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test a backoff calculator/state object with consecutive failure counts (1..N) and a max cap; assert sequence doubles each step and clamps at configured maximum.

behaviors.description: The retry logic correctly identifies a rate-limit response and computes the appropriate wait time before the next attempt.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears at module handoff between response classification and backoff computation. Integration-check quotes: cross-module necessity: 'identifies a rate-limit response and computes the appropriate wait time'; Unit disqualified because a single-function math test cannot verify classifier->scheduler wiring; higher levels disqualified because behavior only requires interpreting a response object and computing delay, not real network/OS. REAL-INFRA: no real HTTP needed; stubbed response payload/status can reproduce failure. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: System considered because this is a REST wrapper, but no phrase requires real HTTP behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire retry controller + rate-limit detector + backoff policy with a stub transport response (e.g., 429 and retry metadata); assert retry branch is taken and computed delay matches policy.

behaviors.description: After the wait, the client actually resends the original request and returns the successful result to the caller transparently.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug is in orchestration across timer/wait module, request executor, and result propagation. Integration-check quotes: cross-module necessity: 'after the wait, the client actually resends the original request and returns the successful result'; Unit disqualified because one function cannot prove original request replay + transparent return path; higher levels disqualified since fake timers and stub transport reproduce this without real OS/network. REAL-INFRA: no subprocess or real network is required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake clock + stub transport (first rate-limited, then success). Assert second send reuses original request payload/params and `call()` resolves to success value without exposing retry internals.

behaviors.description: After the configured maximum number of retries, the client returns a typed `QuotaExhaustedError` rather than a generic error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: typed error emission depends on retry-attempt counting plus terminal error mapping across modules. Integration-check quotes: cross-module necessity: 'after the configured maximum number of retries' and 'returns a typed QuotaExhaustedError'; Unit disqualified because isolated error-class test cannot validate retry loop termination path; higher levels disqualified because deterministic stub failures trigger the same path without real HTTP/OS. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none.
behaviors.test_description: Run `call()` with transport stub that always returns rate-limit failure; set max retries; assert final rejection is `QuotaExhaustedError` type and not a generic/transport error.

behaviors.description: The full retry chain — initial call, backoff retries, quota tracking — terminates correctly and does not loop indefinitely before surfacing the error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is explicit cross-module state-machine behavior across call attempt logic, backoff, and quota tracking. Integration-check quotes: cross-module necessity: 'full retry chain — initial call, backoff retries, quota tracking'; Unit disqualified because single-function tests cannot detect emergent infinite-loop conditions across interacting counters/state; higher levels disqualified because in-process stubs plus bounded fake time can expose non-termination without real OS/network. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: multiple steps exist but not agentic, so not Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with always-rate-limited stub, fake timers, and guard timeout/attempt cap; assert chain stops at expected terminal point and surfaces terminal error instead of continuing indefinitely.

behaviors.description: Requests issued concurrently from multiple async callers in the same process all draw from the same shared quota bucket.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure occurs when concurrent callers interact with shared quota state across modules (client facade, scheduler, quota bucket). Integration-check quotes: cross-module necessity: 'concurrently from multiple async callers' and 'same shared quota bucket'; Unit disqualified because one-function tests cannot reproduce concurrent interleavings and shared state handoff; higher levels disqualified by phrase 'in the same process' (no real distributed/system boundary required). REAL-INFRA: no OS/network dependency to observe bucket-sharing bug. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none.
behaviors.test_description: Start multiple concurrent `call()` invocations against a deterministic stub transport and inspect shared quota manager state/observed admission decisions to verify all callers consume one common bucket.

behaviors.description: When multiple requests are waiting to be sent, they are dispatched in the order they were originally submitted.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: ordering bugs arise at queue + dispatcher + quota-release interaction boundaries, not a single pure function. Integration-check quotes: cross-module necessity: 'multiple requests are waiting to be sent' and 'dispatched in the order they were originally submitted'; Unit disqualified because local queue logic alone cannot verify dispatch timing with async unblocking; higher levels disqualified since deterministic in-process scheduler/timer tests can expose order inversions without real OS/network. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none.
behaviors.test_description: Queue several blocked requests with controlled release conditions (fake timer/quota unlock) and assert observed send order equals submission order under concurrent async callers.
